name: sta-default
description: STA: handoff gate → SDC → tcl → OpenSTA → parse WNS+TNS → report

tasks.detail:
Refuse to run STA on stale or missing netlist:
- [ ] <ip>/syn/out/synth.v exists and non-empty
- [ ] mtime(synth.v) >= max(mtime(<ip>/rtl/*.{sv,v}))
If missing: emit `[STA HANDOFF MISSING] run /syn first` and STOP.
If stale: emit `[STA STALE NETLIST]` and STOP.
Gate: handoff confirmed.

Same Liberty corner as /syn (mismatch silently produces wrong WNS).
- [ ] command -v sta
- [ ] [ -r $SKY130_LIB ]
Gate: tool + PDK both green.

tasks.content: [STA] Read SSOT clocks[]; abort if empty
tasks.detail:
STA without clocks is meaningless.
Extract from <ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml:
- [ ] clocks[] (name, period_ns, source_port)
- [ ] resets[] (for async-reset false paths)
- [ ] false_paths[] (optional)
- [ ] multicycle_paths[] (optional)
Gate: at least one clock parsed.

Run scripts/write_sdc.py <ip>. The SDC must include:
- [ ] one create_clock per SSOT clock
- [ ] set_input_delay / set_output_delay (defaults 20% of period unless SSOT specifies)
- [ ] set_false_path from each async reset port
- [ ] set_false_path / set_multicycle_path entries from SSOT
Gate: SDC file written, contains exactly N create_clock lines.

Run scripts/write_sta_tcl.py <ip>. The tcl must:
- [ ] read_liberty $SKY130_LIB
- [ ] read_verilog <ip>/syn/out/synth.v
- [ ] link_design <top> (top resolved from SSOT)
- [ ] read_sdc <ip>/sta/out/<ip>.sdc
- [ ] report_checks for setup/hold/min_max
- [ ] report_wns / report_tns
Gate: run.tcl exists, references the right netlist + sdc paths.

tasks.content: [STA] Run OpenSTA → sta.log + setup.rpt + hold.rpt + timing.rpt
tasks.detail:
Run scripts/run_opensta.py <ip>.
If rc != 0: tail sta.log, identify error class:
- 'Error: ... not connected' → top mismatch (link_design wrong)
- 'set_false_path: port X not found' → SSOT port name wrong vs synth.v
- 'No constraints' → SDC pathing wrong
Fix and re-run.
Gate: sta rc=0, setup.rpt + hold.rpt non-empty.

Run scripts/parse_wns.py <ip>.
wns.json schema: {top, corner, clocks[{name, period_ns, setup_wns_ns, setup_tns_ns, setup_violations, hold_wns_ns, hold_tns_ns, hold_violations}], summary{all_setup_met, all_hold_met, worst_setup_path, worst_hold_path}}.
Gate: wns.json exists, all SSOT clocks present, no None setup_wns unless rpt is empty.

Run scripts/write_report.py <ip>.
Report must show:
- [ ] result line: PASS / SETUP FAIL / HOLD FAIL
- [ ] per-clock table: period, setup WNS, setup TNS, hold WNS, viol count
- [ ] worst setup + hold path one-liners
- [ ] tool errors/warnings if any
- [ ] next-step hints if FAIL
Gate: sta.report.md written.
